name: Christmas Tiramisu
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Espresso or very strong coffee
9 oz Ladyfingers or 14 oz. Angel Food cake, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, using a fork, beat the cheese until creamy. Set aside.
2. Place a large metal bowl over a pot of boiling water. Using a hand-held electric mixer on medium high, beat the sugar and egg yolks in the bowl for 1 minute, or until well blended. With the mixer on low speed to prevent splashing, gradually add the wine. Continue cooking and beating the mixture, increasing the speed to medium and then to high as the mixture thickens. Cook and beat for 5-7 minutes, or until thickened and light, scraping the sides of the bowl frequently with a rubber spatula.
3. Remove the bowl from the heat and continue beating the mixture for 1 minute longer. Beat in the cheese just until blended (makes about 5 cups).
4. Pour one cup of espresso into a small shallow bowl large enough to hold a ladyfinger horizontally. Dip the rounded top side of each ladyfinger into the espresso, soaking only the top half. If the ladyfingers get too wet, they will fall apart; add more coffee as needed.
5. Place the ladyfingers, flat side down, in the bottom of a 13 x 9 glass or ceramic pan. Do not use a metal pan. Form one layer. Pour half of the custard mixture over the ladyfingers and spread to cover them. Dip the remaining ladyfingers in espresso and form a second layer over the custard mixture. Pour the remaining custard mixture over the ladyfingers and spread evenly.
6. Place cocoa in a strainer and dust the top of the tiramisu evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 10 hours.
Makes: 10-12 servings.
